basics.name: Younghoo Henry Cho
basics.label: Ph.D. Candidate
basics.summary: Ph.D. candidate in Agricultural and Biological Engineering with hands-on experience building data infrastructure, backend services, and verification systems for cross-system data exchange.

work.name: University of Florida
highlights: Develop data infrastructure and verification system prototypes for reusable cross-system data exchange in agriculture.
Build backend services, smart-contract logic, web interfaces, and reproducible technical documentation.
Produce manuscripts, posters, and system artifacts translating research concepts into inspectable and testable prototypes.

work.name: Ministry of Agriculture, Food and Rural Affairs
highlights: Contributed to comparative studies on crop mechanization and digital agriculture development in South Korea and the United States.
Synthesized technical, operational, and policy-side information for cross-country agricultural systems analysis.

work.name: Billion21
highlights: Contributed to AI and computer vision development for marine production and smart aquaculture systems.
Supported applied system development for monitoring, inspection, and automation-oriented workflows.

work.name: Global BioAg Linkages
highlights: Evaluated technical and data requirements for deploying AI in agricultural production systems.

work.name: Texas Tech University
highlights: Supported research projects in computer vision and data visualization.

publications.name: DEMETER: A Blockchain-Based Platform for Tracking the History of Shared Agricultural Data Files
Facilitating a Future Agricultural Data Ecosystem: A Cross-Sectoral Review of Blockchain Application in Data Sharing
Bridging carbon markets and agriculture: A comparative study of stakeholder engagement in South Korea and United States

certificates.name: Patent: Efficient Seaweed Farming Data Management by Utilizing Blockchain
Patent: Methods for Selecting Spores from Laver Farm Devices
